# READ THESE THREE FILES FIRST, IN FULL. THEY ARE YOUR FOUNDATION — BUILD ON THEM, DO NOT RE-DERIVE THEM.
1. /home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/foundation/SOL_PLAN_V4.md      — GPT-5.6 Sol's complete plan (coverage matrix, retrieval chain, working-paper
                                  text lane, the arithmetic: we need 1.62x the judge's points, INSIGHT alone
                                  needs 1.81x; the wheel; kill rules; three real bugs in our code).
2. /home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/foundation/FABLE_PLAN_V4.md    — Fable's complete plan (four sentence classes; mechanism-pool scoping; corpus-
                                  scoped gap claims; integrity gates) **PLUS ITS VERBATIM DECODE OF cellcog, the
                                  #1 system — the most valuable artifact we have.**
3. /home/polaris/polaris_project/sota_review/foundation/OPERATOR_FIXES.md   — **SEVEN CORRECTIONS NEITHER DESIGNER FOUND. NOT OPTIONAL.** The recency fix
                                  (date-filtered search — retires Sol's biggest risk); SELECT is the broken stage;
                                  scope must be DERIVED from the prompt; **BOTH PLANS ARE OVERFIT**; the
                                  faithfulness line; the load-bearing unknown; three critical code bugs.

Also available: /home/polaris/polaris_project/SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md (all measurements), the winners' artifacts at /home/polaris/polaris_project/drb_corpus/gpt55_board,
our code at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel, and the grader's own source at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/third_party/deep_research_bench/.



# THE MISSION
Build a **research system that beats the state of the art on ANY question** — not a machine that answers task 72.
POLARIS = 0.4382. Highest KNOWN task-72 score = bodhi 0.5441. cellcog is #1 corpus-wide at 0.5578 (its task-72
score is unpublished; regression estimates ~0.556 +/- 0.019). Weights: INSIGHT .32 | COMPREH .29 | INSTR .25 | READ .14.
Judge noise SD 0.0074 => k=5 paired resolves +0.0094. NEVER score n=1.

# THE THREE HARD CONSTRAINTS ON YOUR DESIGN
1. **NOT OVERFIT.** Sol and Fable BOTH hardcode 15 anchor papers, a 10-section outline, an 8x10 coverage matrix
   and a topic regex — all specific to AI-and-labour. The operator caught it. **A system that answers one question
   is a demo.** Every constant must be DERIVED FROM THE PROMPT at runtime.
   **KEYSTONE INSIGHT: RACE's own judge GENERATES its 25 grading criteria from the task prompt
   (third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py — the generator is handed only the task text).
   We can generate our COVERAGE MATRIX the same way, from the same prompt.**
2. **FAITHFULNESS IS ON THE TABLE — the operator has authorised relaxing it.** But get the line right:
   fabrication (putting words in a source's mouth) must stay impossible; the reviewer's own reasoning, MARKED AS
   SUCH, is what every literature review does and what the #1 system does constantly (71% of its sentences carry
   no citation). **The moat is not "every sentence must be span-grounded" — it is "no sentence may put words in a
   source's mouth."** Keep Fable's refinements (mechanism pool = the sentence's OWN cited cards; hedge+tag alone
   is costume; gap claims must be corpus-scoped).
3. **ADDRESS EVERY GAP AT ONCE** — retrieval, select, reasoning, composition, measurement — and be HONEST about
   the load-bearing unknown: **we have proven the judge can SEE our scholarship. We have NOT proven it PAYS for it.**


# YOUR ANGLE
**BLANK SHEET, THEN MAP BACK.** Read the foundation. Then set it aside and ask: if you were building a deep-research system today, from nothing, to beat every system on DeepResearch Bench ACROSS ALL 100 TASKS — what is its architecture? THEN map it back: what of Sol/Fable/our code survives, what dies, what must be built. The operator has authorised dropping ANY existing rule. Be radical where radical is right, and say so where it is not.

# RULES
- **THINK DEEPLY.** This is the most important design work in the project. Take the time you need.
- **BUILD ON THE FOUNDATION.** Sol and Fable did real work — take it, improve it, and say what you rejected.
  Do not re-derive what they already established.
- **VERIFY anything load-bearing.** Every brief in this project has been wrong at least once, including mine.
- **NO PROXIES.** We lost a full night to metrics that moved while the mechanism never fired.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1	# SOL (GPT-5.6) — FINAL v4 PLAN. This is FOUNDATION. Build ON it; do not re-derive it.
2	
3	## DIAGNOSIS
4	FOUR REFUTATIONS, ADJUDICATED AGAINST THE ARTIFACTS. I accept all four factually; I reject the conclusion that any of them forces a retreat to parity.
5	
6	R1 BAR-LOWERING — CONCEDED, FATAL, FIXED. `sota_review/codex_plan_v3.md:368` literally reads "Mean >=0.5441 across independent compositions" — bodhi's score, the exact number SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:10 forbids ("NOT parity. NOT bodhi. THE TOP."). Its ceiling table (382-383) prices the full build at 0.53-0.56 and leaves >0.5578 to an adjective ("Excellent execution"). That plan is dead. New release gate: 0.5670 (target + ~1 sigma of the combined SE the plan itself computed, ~0.010), because cellcog's TASK-72 score is unpublished — only the corpus-wide 0.5578 exists — so the true bar may be above 0.5578. Gating AT 0.5578 is a coin flip; gating at 0.5441 is a designed loss.
7	
8	R2 FABRICATION — CONCEDED, TWO REAL LEAKS, BOTH ON THE CRITICAL PATH. Verified by grep: `scripts/synthesis_contract.py` is imported at `scripts/cellcog_composer.py:49` and `validate()` is NEVER CALLED anywhere. The only thing between LLM prose and the page is `_clean()` (cellcog_composer.py:342, called at :387) — a regex strip. Second: cellcog_composer.py:161 span-checks `claim`/`span` against source text, but line 167 copies `mechanisms` in as free-form LLM output — an unverified mechanism string becomes a global citation license. Third: the abstract at cellcog_composer.py:400-414 is HARD-CODED and asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" and a fixed Findings paragraph, emitted regardless of what the cards say. That is a fabricated compliance claim aimed at the instruction-following grader. All three are fixed below. But R2's demand to re-narrow the mechanism rule is only half right: bodhi's own winning sentence HEDGES ("a pattern consistent with GPT diffusion lags", SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:66-68). So hedge + name-both-sources costs us NOTHING and closes the leak. I take the hedge, I keep the cross-source move.
9	
10	R3 JUDGE-INVISIBILITY — CONCEDED AS A CONSTRAINT, NOT A REFUTATION. Confirmed at drb_corpus/gpt55_board/leaderboard_gpt55.csv: citation_accuracy and effective_citations are "-" for all ten systems. RACE judges cleaned prose only. Consequence, now a hard design rule: NO lever may bank its value in [n] markers, bibliography, or retrieval provenance. Every lever below is stated as a prose artifact that survives marker/bibliography deletion.
11	
12	R4 CORPUS CEILING — CONCEDED ON THE NUMBERS, REJECTED ON THE INFERENCE. I re-counted outputs/journal_corpus_content.json myself: 70 rows, FULLTEXT 12 / ABSTRACT_ONLY 24 / CITATION_ONLY 34. Two of the 12 are repository landing pages. Real quotable full text ~10. So "~100 canonical journal articles with usable full text" IS a wish and is deleted. But R4 then concludes we must "re-plan for a bodhi-shaped corpus" — that is the bar-lowering move wearing a lab coat, and it rests on a false premise: THE JUDGE NEVER SEES FULL TEXT. RACE grades "Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized" from the PROSE. The unit the prose needs is not a 40-page PDF; it is a citable finding: author + journal + year + a number + a stated design. Economics abstracts carry exactly that ("one more robot per thousand workers reduces the employment-to-population ratio by 0.2 pp"). ABSTRACT_ONLY is therefore a first-class evidence tier, not a shortfall — and it SCALES, because Crossref abstracts are free and 200-answering. The full-text bottleneck binds only on mechanism spans and method contrasts, where ~15-25 quotable papers is sufficient. Ground truth on what we are chasing: cellcog's task-72 artifact is 16,334 words, 31 H3, 10 table rows, and 164 mentions of 2023/2024/2025. Recency is the real axis R4 identified correctly, and it is the one place we can genuinely lose.
13	
14	## RETRIEVAL
15	RETRIEVAL IS REBUILT AT THE ROOT — IT ADDS SOURCES, IT DOES NOT FILTER THE 105. codex_plan_v3.md:347 ("Canonicalize and classify all 105 existing rows") is corpus hygiene masquerading as retrieval; you cannot filter 45 on-topic papers into 100+. Deleted.
16	
17	STEP 0 — TOOL PREFLIGHT, A HARD GATE BEFORE ANY BUILD (new: scripts/retrieval_preflight.py). R4 proved OpenAlex 429s and Semantic Scholar 404s from this IP. 429 is a rate limit, not a ban: retry through the OpenAlex polite pool (mailto=aldrin.or@c-polarbiotech.com, 1 req/s, exponential backoff, 5 retries). The gate is: a scripted loop must return HTTP 200 on 20 consecutive forward-citation lookups. Fallbacks in order: (a) OpenAlex polite pool; (b) Semantic Scholar with an API key; (c) Crossref `/works/{doi}` `reference[]` array — Crossref DOES return deposited reference lists, which gives BACKWARD graph expansion for free at 200 OK, plus `is-referenced-by-count` for canonicity ranking. If none of (a)/(b) answers 200, FORWARD expansion is declared UNAVAILABLE, post-2023 coverage is at risk, and that fact goes straight into the kill rule below — it is not papered over.
18	
19	STEP 1 — ANCHOR SEEDING (15 canonical anchors, hand-named, not searched): Autor-Levy-Murnane QJE 2003; Autor JEP 2015; Acemoglu-Restrepo AER 2018, JPE 2020, JEP 2019; Goos-Manning-Salomons AER 2014; Frey-Osborne TFSC 2017; Bresnahan-Brynjolfsson-Hitt QJE 2002; Brynjolfsson-Li-Raymond QJE 2025; Noy-Zhang Science 2023; Dell'Acqua et al. (field experiment); Felten-Raj-Seamans SMJ; Webb; Eloundou et al. Science 2024; Agrawal-Gans-Goldfarb JEP 2019. SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:98-105 is right that keyword search cannot find ALM 2003 (its title contains neither "AI" nor "labor market") — so we do not search for the canon, we NAME it.
20	
21	STEP 2 — CITATION-GRAPH EXPANSION TO DEPTH 2 (reuse scripts/journal_corpus_build.py, which already works). Backward via Crossref references; forward via OpenAlex `cited_by_api_url` (this is the ONLY way to reach 2023-2026 generative-AI journal papers from 2003-2019 anchors — R4 is correct that backward-only expansion is time-reversed by construction and produced zero 2024-2026 papers).
22	
23	STEP 3 — THE AGENTIC LOOP, ACTUALLY RUN (PG_AGENTIC_*: 15 rounds, 120 queries; scripts/outline_agentic_sweep.py + the pg_loopback_* harnesses). Query generation is JOURNAL-FIRST and MATRIX-DIRECTED: every round reads the coverage matrix and generates queries only for EMPTY CELLS ("radiology AND employment AND journal", "generative AI AND customer service agents AND field experiment"). This is the query-gen -> search -> fetch -> select -> weight -> dedup -> consolidate chain the operator named, aimed at holes instead of at a keyword.
24	
25	STEP 4 — THE STOPPING CONDITION IS THE COVERAGE MATRIX, NOT A NUMBER (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md §8.1; new: scripts/coverage_matrix.py). Rows = the restructuring dimensions the rubric names VERBATIM (race_task72_criteria.txt: "job creation, job displacement, job transformation, changes in skill demands, wage effects, and productivity changes" + inequality + organizational adaptation = 8). Columns = 10 industries (manufacturing, healthcare, finance/insurance, retail/logistics, transport, professional/legal, software/IT, education, customer service/BPO, agriculture/construction) — because "Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis" is w=0.25 INSIDE comprehensiveness and "Coverage of Various Industries" is w=0.15 inside instruction-following. A cell is FILLED when >=2 independent journal studies each supply a number + a stated design. SATURATION = >=80% of the 80 cells filled, NO empty row, NO empty column, and >=1 post-2022 study in >=6 industries. Retrieval halts when two consecutive agentic rounds add <3 newly filled cells. Expected terminal corpus: 110-160 distinct on-topic journal DOIs (vs cellcog's ~98), of which >=90 carry >=1 admitted card.
26	
27	ADMISSION RULE (replaces the blanket working-paper reject at codex_plan_v3.md:114, which can only shrink us): a DOI enters if (venue is a JOURNAL) AND (an LLM topic judge, not a regex, rates it on-topic against the task prompt) AND (is-referenced-by-count or venue quality clears a floor). The 120-DOI pool in outputs/corpus_journal_evidence.json (~58% off-topic: BMJ, PRISMA, ImageNet, Youth & Society 1986) is KILLED as an evidence source and re-derived through this gate.
28	
29	THE WORKING-PAPER LANE — the unlock R4 missed. Unpaywall cannot reach the econ canon (ALM 2003 is_oa=false; AER 2018's "OA" URL is a 403 Cloudflare wall). But NBER/IZA/arXiv/author PDFs carry the SAME PAPER. Rule: we CITE ONLY THE JOURNAL VERSION in prose (the instruction-following criterion "Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles", w=0.15, is graded on what the judge READS, and it reads "Writing in the QJE in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane..."); we use the working-paper PDF ONLY as text substrate for span-checking mechanism and method claims; and NO NUMBER may be quoted from a working-paper PDF — numbers must come from the published abstract or full text, because version drift is real. This gets the canon's TEXT without citing a non-journal source.
30	
31	## EVIDENCE_LAYER
32	THE CARD IS THE ATOM, AND THE TIER SYSTEM IS HONEST (rewrite of scripts/cellcog_composer.py:130-175 into scripts/evidence_cards.py).
33	
34	FIX THE FULLTEXT DETECTOR FIRST — it is a lying measurement. scripts/journal_corpus_fetch.py accepted a 548-word Oxford ORA landing page ("CONTACT Name Email... We welcome feedback") as FULLTEXT for Frey-Osborne and a 491-word bepress page for Tallon 2019. New guard: >=1500 words AND title appears in the body AND no landing-page chrome. FULLTEXT then correctly drops 12 -> 10.
35	
36	THREE TIERS, THREE PROOF OBLIGATIONS, ALL THREE COUNTS PUBLISHED (never again "70 sources" or "36 usable"):
37	- QUOTABLE (fulltext >=1500 real words; today ~10, target 25-40 via the working-paper text lane): may carry mechanism spans, method contrasts, RANK_EVIDENCE, CONTRASTS_METHOD, and any calc manifest.
38	- ABSTRACT-CITABLE (today 24, target 80-120): may carry exactly the findings STATED VERBATIM IN THE ABSTRACT — author, journal, year, point estimate, design. This is the workhorse tier and it is what "Depth and Representativeness" is actually graded on, because the judge reads prose, not PDFs.
39	- NAMED (citation-only): may appear in the Scope/literature narrative and be counted in the corpus, NEVER in a claim.
40	
41	CARD FIELDS, ALL DECLARED, ALL VERIFIED: claim, span (span-checked against source text — this gate already exists and works at cellcog_composer.py:158-161), level (task/occupation/firm/industry/economy), horizon (contemporaneous/short/long), method (RCT, field experiment, diff-in-diff, IV, panel, simulation, survey, task-mapping), technology-era (pre-2010 IT / robots / pre-LLM ML / generative AI), population/country, outcome family, number, year, venue, authors, attribution string ("Writing in the QJE in 2003, Autor, Levy and Murnane show...").
42	
43	MECHANISM FIELD IS NOW SPAN-CHECKED — R2's leak closed at source. cellcog_composer.py:167 currently copies `'mechanisms': f.get('mechanisms') or []` straight from LLM output with no verification, while claim/span next to it ARE checked. A hallucinated mechanism string is a global assertion license (two-hop laundering: invent the mechanism field, then "cite" its paper). New rule: every mechanism carries its OWN verbatim span and is dropped if the span is not in the source text. QUOTABLE tier only — an abstract cannot license a mechanism.
44	
45	DEDUP: canonical DOI (lowercased, prefix-normalized), plus title-similarity clustering for WP/journal version pairs, which are merged into ONE work carrying the journal identity and the WP text.
46	WEIGHTING: rank by (topic-relevance score) x (venue tier) x log(citations) x recency-bonus for >=2023 — the recency bonus is deliberate, because cellcog's task-72 report mentions 2023/2024/2025 164 times and bodhi only 11, and cellcog is #1.
47	
48	## SYNTHESIS_LANE
49	THE CORRECTED LINE, CORRECTED AGAIN — AND IT COSTS US NOTHING.
50	
51	SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:74 says "A mechanism may be asserted if ANY source in the corpus states it AND we cite that source." R2 is right that as a bare string-membership test this admits "The undetectable aggregate effect of generative AI is DRIVEN BY the reinstatement effect" with a 2019 robot-theory paper as the license — a new empirical claim about the world, delivered under two prestigious journal names. That is fabrication and it must not ship.
52	
53	But look at what bodhi ACTUALLY wrote (SOTA_BRIEF_V4.md:66-68): "...aggregate impacts were too small to detect over the period studied [3] — A PATTERN CONSISTENT WITH GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [1]." bodhi HEDGES. It does not assert causation. So the hedge is free: it is exactly the sentence that beat us.
54	
55	WHAT A SENTENCE MAY ASSERT (new operation MECHANISM_CONSISTENCY in scripts/synthesis_contract.py, replacing the widening of rule 6):
56	1. A finding from paper A, verbatim-grounded in A's admitted span, attributed to A in prose with journal + year.
57	2. A mechanism from paper B, verbatim-grounded in B's OWN span (QUOTABLE tier only), attributed to B IN THE SAME SENTENCE with journal + year, so the mechanism is never asserted under A's name.
58	3. The link between them ONLY as a hedged consistency relation: "a pattern consistent with", "which would be expected under", "the theory predicts". BANNED, deterministically: "driven by", "caused by", "attributable to", "reflects", "explains", "the mechanism at work is", "operates through", "accounts for", "the channel is". CAUSAL_IMPORT (synthesis_contract.py:79-81) is a fixed trigger-word regex today and misses half of these — it gets rewritten as a banned-verb list plus a dependency check for cross-premise causal predicates.
59	4. TYPED SETTING-MATCH, enforced on declared card fields, not substrings: B's mechanism may be attached to A's finding only if technology-era, level, and outcome-family are COMPATIBLE. This is what kills R2's admitted attack sentences: Bresnahan 2002 (era = pre-2010 office IT) cannot be attached to a 2022 generative-AI null (era = generative AI) — the typed check rejects it before any regex sees it. R2's three admitted sentences go into ATTACKS as must-REJECT cases; the suite is not green until they fail.
60	
61	STILL DELETED, DETERMINISTICALLY: any number not in an admitted span; any study/organisation/person that does not exist; any attribution to someone who did not say it; any mechanism no paper states; forecasts; universals; and now, any causal predicate spanning two premises.
62	
63	THE GATE IS ON THE CRITICAL PATH — THIS IS THE FIX THAT MATTERS. Verified: `validate()` is imported at cellcog_composer.py:49 and CALLED NOWHERE. The only filter is `_clean()` (defined :342, called :387) — a regex strip that the composer then PRINTS as "sentences DROPPED by the contract". Faithfulness today is enforced by asking the model nicely. Fix: the composer emits a STRUCTURED SENTENCE LEDGER (kind, premise_ids, operation, rendered text); every sentence goes through validate() before assembly; failures are DELETED and logged; the run aborts if >15% of sentences fail (that means the prompt is wrong, not the prose). The repo has already recorded this exact failure twice — commits 225b323 ("mechanism never operated") and b3acb72 ("the judge fell off the critical path"). Third time it gets a test: a CI assertion that a poisoned card produces zero poisoned sentences on the page.
64	
65	THE HARD-CODED ABSTRACT DIES (cellcog_composer.py:400-414). It asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed, English-language journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" and a fixed Findings paragraph, unconditioned on the cards. That is a fabricated compliance claim aimed straight at the instruction-following grader. The abstract is DERIVED from admitted cards, and the Scope section may state only what a corpus audit proves at compose time (counts are read from the audit JSON, not typed by hand).
66	
67	## COMPOSER
68	TABLE STAKES (matching cellcog's shape is NOT the win — say so out loud, then do it): 9 H2 / 32-36 H3 / 6-8 H4; median paragraph 90-110 words (we are at 677 — the worst readability on the board, 0.3774, and the judge scores all 25 criteria in ONE comparative call, so this taxes the other 86% of the score); 13,500-16,500 body words (cellcog's task-72 artifact measures 16,334); a structured abstract (Objective/Methods/Findings/Contributions — survives the cleaner in full); epistemic labels ([Established finding] / [Contested] / [Emerging] — cellcog is the ONLY system on the board that does this); >=1 summary table of key studies (cellcog has 10 table rows; readability criterion D1 explicitly rewards "a summary table of key studies and their findings"); ZERO [n] markers in prose and ZERO parenthetical (YYYY) — every attribution in FORMAT D ("Writing in the American Economic Review in 2020, Acemoglu and Restrepo find..."), the only format that survived the production cleaner (10/12 authors, 12/12 journals, vs 0/12 for markers and 0/12 for parenthetical author-year). No meta-commentary in the opening — the current report opens "This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence on the question above" when there is no question above, and the judge reads the opening most carefully.
69	
70	THE 4IR SPINE (this is 11.5% of the score and we drop it after paragraph one). "4IR" is graded three times: comprehensiveness "Grounding in AI and the 4IR Context" (w=0.10), insight "Insightful Integration of AI's Role within the 4IR Context" (w=0.15, and the criterion text explicitly punishes "merely mentioning the 4IR superficially"), instruction-following "Integration of AI as a Key Driver of the 4IR" (w=0.15). 13	THE CORRECTED LINE, WITH SCOPING RESTORED. A sentence may assert exactly one of four things: CLASS 1 REPORT — a finding ENTAILED by an extractive span of a card it cites. CLASS 3 SCOPE-DIFFERENCE — a hedged FACT ABOUT THE CARDS ('these estimates observe different units of analysis'); it may NEVER assert the metadata diff EXPLAINS the divergence — a diff on `level` proves the levels differ, not causation (the last plan's own exemplar violated this; fixed). CI GATE: scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test must keep ATTACK #1 REJECTED on every commit. NEUTRAL IS NOT A ROUTER: a NEUTRAL verdict from entailment_judge.py triggers rewrite-and-re-judge (max 2: rewrite to be entailed, or re-route to a class that supplies premises and re-validate); a sentence that cannot pass any class WITH premises is DELETED, as today. 24	## CEILING_HONESTY
25	THE ONE THRESHOLD IS 0.5578 + 0.0094. cellcog's task-72 row is unpublished (its scores/ dir ships only race_result.txt — verified); the regression band for its task-72 runs ~0.537-0.575, and this plan is built for the 0.575 BRANCH (design point 0.572, wheel continues past 0.5578 while levers still clear +0.0094), not the convenient 0.556 branch. WHERE IT CAN HONESTLY FALL SHORT: (a) insight 0.58 requires the judge to reward adjudication over assertion at band-jump magnitude — no system has measured above 0.5708 on this dimension; (b) comprehensiveness superiority rests on judge-visible attribution density and matrix breadth, not groundable-text superiority, because the measured funnel (journal_corpus_content.json: 36/70 groundable; 2024+ lane ~47% usable text) caps us at ~60-75 groundable cards — grounding parity with cellcog, not the 1.5x the last plan booked. If both stall at ~0.555 the total is ~0.557 and the report says MISSED, with those two numbers and the closers named above. THE ASSUMPTION WE ARE STILL MAKING AND HAVE NOT TESTED — stated plainly: that the GPT-5.5 judge PAYS for visible scholarship at the magnitude this arithmetic requires. Everything measured so far is about what SURVIVES the cleaner (tags 7/7, format-E years 10/12, headings 5/5) — feature VISIBILITY — not about what the judge pays for those features — feature VALUE. We have never composed one full report in this architecture and scored it k=5; the visibility-to-value conversion rate is the plan's load-bearing unknown, and wheel iteration 1 exists to measure it before anything else is tuned. Second-order untested: COCI coverage for the non-economics matrix cells (its index is Crossref-open-references only), and whether a 15-16k-word draft survives the strict gates without a deletion cascade shrinking it below the length target — gate survival at this scale has never been run.

27	## KILL_RULE
28	THREE LAYERS, NONE NEGOTIABLE. (1) LEVER KILL: k=5 paired scoring only (SD 0.0074); a lever is kept iff its paired delta >= +0.0094; a lever that fails once is reverted immediately — no tweak-and-retry of a dead lever. (2) RUN KILL: if two consecutive wheel iterations gain < +0.0094 total while the mean sits below 0.5578, STOP and report MISSED with the per-dimension gaps and the named closers (full-text channel, attribution density, readability) — no renarration, no 'beat every measured score' consolation sentence; the ONLY permitted success statement is 'mean k=5 >= 0.5578 + 0.0094 with all integrity gates green'. (3) INTEGRITY KILL (any red blocks scoring and ships nothing): scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test must keep ATTACK #1 ('imports a mechanism no premise states') REJECTED — the CI canary that the mechanism-pool scoping was never widened; cleaner_survival_test.py on the actual draft must pass its four assertions; the unscoped-negative-existential regex must return zero; the attribution binding gate must return zero mismatches; and any single fabricated number, entity, attribution, or mechanism found in the line-by-line READ kills the draft outright regardless of its score — the verified-grounding moat is the product; a high score obtained by breaking it is a loss.

22	**BUT — AND THIS IS THE REAL LESSON:** that same query also returns scGPT, convolutional neural networks,
23	vision-language models and medical image segmentation. **Date-filtering gets us RECENT papers; it does not get
24	us RELEVANT ones.** Which means:
25	
26	## FIX 2 — **SELECT IS THE BROKEN STAGE, NOT RETRIEVAL.**
27	Our corpus: 997 rows, 919 URLs, 206 DOIs — and after enrichment only **17 of 120 journal works are ON-TOPIC**.
28	It contains **ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist, and a 1974 Cognitive Psychology paper on reading automaticity.**
29	Prestigious. Peer-reviewed. Utterly irrelevant.
30	
31	**RETRIEVAL WAS NEVER SHALLOW. IT WAS AIMED WRONG.** More search with the same broken SELECT just yields more
32	junk — and now, *recent* junk. SELECT must be an **LLM relevance judge against the actual research question**,
33	never a regex, never a venue-prestige score. (`topic_relevance_gate` already exists in the codebase.)
34	Prestige is not relevance. Tier is a WEIGHT, never a FILTER.

38	runtime from the question itself:
39	  - **topic** (what is this actually about?)
40	  - **recency window** (a 2026 AI question needs 2024+; a French-Revolution question does not)
41	  - **venue class** ("only cites high-quality journal articles" => journals only; other prompts differ)
42	  - **language**

44	**This is what makes it a system rather than a hack: the same code answers a clinical question, a financial one,

73	- **SCHOLARSHIP = the reviewer's own reasoning, marked as such.** "We propose, as analytical synthesis, that..."

# Four evaluation dimensions for final article quality
# 1. Comprehensiveness: Article covers key areas of the industry, ensures overall understanding, doesn't omit important parts
# 2. Insight/Depth: Article deeply analyzes causes, impacts and trends, provides valuable insights
# 3. Instruction-Following/Relevance: Article closely follows research topic, directly answers questions
# 4. Readability: Article has clear structure, fluent language, and is easy to understand

# Prompt to generate evaluation dimension weights
generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt = """
<system_role>
You are an experienced research article evaluation expert. You excel at deeply understanding the objectives, challenges, and core value points of specific research tasks, and based on this, setting **dynamic, reasonable, and well-supported** dimension weights for subsequent article quality assessment.
</system_role>

<user_prompt>
There is a deep research task as follows:
<task>
"{task_prompt}"
</task>

<instruction>
**Background**: The research team will conduct in-depth and comprehensive research based on the `<task>` above and ultimately produce a high-quality research article.
**Your Task**: As an evaluation expert, you need to set the evaluation criteria weights for this specific `<task>` for our assessment team. The evaluation will be conducted across the following four dimensions:
1.  **Comprehensiveness:** The breadth, depth, and relevance of information coverage.
2.  **Insight:** The depth, originality, logic, and value of the analysis and conclusions.
3.  **Instruction Following:** Whether the report accurately and completely responds to all requirements and constraints of the task.
4.  **Readability:** Clarity of structure, fluency of language, effectiveness of data presentation, and overall ease of understanding.

3	def calculate_weighted_scores(llm_output_json, criteria_data, language="en"):
4	    """
5	    Calculates weighted scores based on LLM output and criteria weights.
6	    
7	    Args:
8	        llm_output_json: JSON output from LLM with scoring data
9	        criteria_data: Criteria configuration with dimension weights
10	        language: Language of the evaluation (default: "en")
11	        
12	    Returns:
13	        Dictionary with weighted scores for target and reference models
14	    """
15	    results = {
16	        "target": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0},
17	        "reference": {"dims": {}, "total": 0.0}
18	    }
19	    total_target_score = 0.0
20	    total_reference_score = 0.0
21	    
22	    # Get dimension weights
23	    dimension_weights = criteria_data.get("dimension_weight", {})
24	    task_id = criteria_data.get("id", "Unknown") 
25	    
26	    # Check if criterions exist
27	    if "criterions" not in criteria_data or not criteria_data["criterions"]:
28	        error_msg = f"ID: {task_id} - Missing required criterions data, cannot calculate weighted scores"
29	        logging.error(error_msg)
30	        raise ValueError(error_msg)
31	    
32	    # Create a mapping from criterion text to weight for easier lookup
33	    criterion_weights = {}
34	    for dim, criterions in criteria_data.get("criterions", {}).items():
35	        criterion_weights[dim] = {crit['criterion']: crit['weight'] for crit in criterions}
36	
37	    # Record all unmatched criteria for warnings
38	    unmatched_criteria = set()
39	    
40	    for dim, scores_list in llm_output_json.items():
41	        if not isinstance(scores_list, list):
42	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' in LLM output is not a list. Skipping.")
43	            continue
44	
45	        if dim not in dimension_weights:
46	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria dimension weights. Skipping dimension.")
47	            continue
48	            
49	        if dim not in criterion_weights:
50	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Dimension '{dim}' from LLM output not found in criteria details. Skipping dimension.")
51	            continue
52	
53	        dim_target_weighted_sum = 0.0
54	        dim_reference_weighted_sum = 0.0
55	        dim_total_weight = 0.0
56	
57	        dim_criteria_map = criterion_weights.get(dim, {})
58	        
59	        # Skip dimension if no criteria mapping exists
60	        if not dim_criteria_map:
61	            logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - No criteria mapping found for dimension '{dim}'. Skipping dimension.")
62	            continue
63	
64	        for score_item in scores_list:
65	            if not isinstance(score_item, dict):
66	                logging.warning(f"ID: {task_id} - Item in scores_list for dimension '{dim}' is not a dictionary. Skipping item: {score_item}")
67	                continue
68	
69	            criterion_text_raw = score_item.get("criterion")
70	            criterion_text = criterion_text_raw.strip() if isinstance(criterion_text_raw, str) else None
71	
72	            # Check different score field formats
73	            # 1. Comparative scoring mode: article_1_score and article_2_score

input.thesis:
The grader is not a black box we must guess at — it is a **queryable oracle we already own**, and we have been throwing away its answer. RACE generates its criteria AND its dimension weights from the task prompt alone, scores our draft against a fixed reference on ~25 criteria at 0-10, and emits a written comparative analysis for each one — then `deepresearch_bench_race.py:186` discards all of it and keeps five floats. Build the whole system as a closed loop around that discarded output: compile the prompt into the grader's own rubric, let the rubric (not an engineer) derive the corpus targets, the outline, and the stopping rule, and let the judge's per-criterion deficit map — not a scalar — decide what to fix next.

## THE ARITHMETIC THAT CHANGES THE DESIGN (all recomputed from raw artifacts tonight)

**1. The score is a ratio against a fixed reference.** `deepresearch_bench_race.py:155-160`: `overall_score = target_total / (target_total + reference_total)`. The reference is `data/test_data/cleaned_data/reference.jsonl` — 100 articles, one per task, task-72's is 9,029 words / 8 H2 / 24 H3. Since R is a constant, **maximising S is exactly maximising T = Σ_c W_c · T_c**, where W_c = dim_weight × criterion_weight and T_c ∈ [0,10]. That is a linear objective over 25 named, weighted, individually-scored components. We have never once looked at those components.

**2. Nobody has ever exceeded 1.27x the reference.** Converting every board system's task-72 dims to points-ratio T/R = r/(1-r):

```
                       compr  insight  instr   read   overall
bodhi                  1.194   1.201   1.252  1.083   1.193
lunon                  1.191   1.270   1.138  1.015   1.177
WhaleCloud             1.182   1.188   1.171  1.120   1.172
dalpha                 1.089   1.083   1.151  1.120   1.106
gemini-2.5-pro-DR      1.046   1.014   1.078  1.041   1.042
POLARIS (us)                                          0.780
```
The entire competitive frontier lives in a band of 1.08–1.27. The single best dimension anyone has ever posted on this task is lunon's insight at 1.270. **Sol's release gate (0.5670) and Fable's (0.5672) both require T/R = 1.31 — higher than any system has ever achieved on any dimension of this task.** Neither designer converted their gate into the units the metric actually uses, so neither noticed they had set the bar above the observed frontier.

**3. Therefore the single-lever kill rule in BOTH plans is statistically incapable of seeing a lever, and this is why the project keeps burning nights on "metrics that moved while the mechanism never fired."** ∂S/∂T = R/(T+R)². With our S = 0.4382 and a reference scoring ~7/10, that is ≈0.045 overall points per raw weighted point. A criterion of weight W_c = 0.0435 (typical: comprehensiveness 0.29 × criterion 0.15) must move **+4.8 points on the 0-10 scale** — half the entire scale, on one criterion — to produce the +0.0094 that both plans demand before keeping a lever. **Both plans mandate one-lever-at-a-time AND a kill rule that cannot resolve one lever. They would have killed every good lever they built.** Measured at the criterion level instead, where the lever actually acts, a lever that moves its own criterion by 1-3 points is resolvable at roughly 3-8 sigma at k=5. Same judge calls, same cost — we just stop deleting the output. That is a ~10x gain in statistical power for free, and it is the methodological unlock the project has been missing.

**4. The load-bearing unknown is not unknowable — it is one afternoon away.** Both plans build for weeks on a *regression estimate* of cellcog's task-72 score (0.556 ± 0.019) and set their gates off it. But we have cellcog's task-72 article, we have the reference, and we have the judge. **Just score it.** The same run also yields R_total — which fixes the analytic ceiling at 10/(10+R_total) (R=7.0 → 0.588; R=7.5 → 0.571) and tells us whether Sol's 0.5670 gate is even *reachable*. This is the cheapest, highest-value hour in the project and neither plan contains it.
input.design:
## THE SYSTEM: SIX STAGES, ZERO DOMAIN CONSTANTS

Everything below is derived from the prompt at runtime. Nothing names a paper, an industry, a section, or a topic.

---

### STAGE 0 — THE RUBRIC COMPILER (new: `scripts/rubric_compile.py`)
**Input: the task prompt. Nothing else.**

Run the grader's own generators, verbatim, from `third_party/deep_research_bench/prompt/criteria_prompt_en.py` (and `_zh.py` — 27 of the 100 tasks are Chinese; language is selected from the prompt):
- `generate_eval_dimension_weight_prompt` → the four dimension weights.
- the four `generate_eval_criteria_prompt_{comp,insight,instruction,readability}` → ~25 criteria, each with `{criterion, explanation, weight}`.

Run k=3, merge. Output `rubric.json`, byte-identical in schema to `data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl`.

**Then FACET EXPANSION — this is where the coverage matrix comes from.** For each criterion, one LLM call with the criterion text + its `explanation` + the prompt: *"enumerate the specific things a report must cover for an expert to score this 10/10."* The union of facets across criteria **is** the coverage matrix. Rows are criteria (derived), columns are facets (derived). A cell is FILLED when ≥2 admitted cards support it.

**This replaces, and kills, Sol's hand-built 8×10 matrix and Fable's 9×6 matrix.** Both are hardcoded to AI-and-labour; the operator was right that they are demos.

**IT IS FALSIFIABLE BEFORE WE BUILD ANYTHING.** The benchmark ships the real criteria for all 100 tasks (`data/criteria_data/criteria.jsonl`, verified: 100 rows, `dimension_weight` + `criterions`). Run the compiler on all 100 prompts and measure semantic recall of the real criteria + correlation of weights. **This is the anti-overfit proof, and it is executable.** If recall < ~80% the keystone is weak and we fall back to the static rubric in `score_prompt_en.py:104-194` as a floor — we learn that in a day, not a month.

---

### STAGE 1 — SCOPE DERIVATION (new: `scripts/scope_derive.py`) — operator FIX 3
One LLM call, prompt → `scope.json`:
- `topic_statement` (the SELECT gate's yardstick)
- `venue_class` — **derived from the prompt's own words.** Task 72 says "only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles" → hard journal gate. **"What caused the 2008 financial crisis" says no such thing → the FCIC report, BIS papers and Gorton's book are ADMISSIBLE.** Sol's blanket journal-only rule would be catastrophic there. This is precisely why it must be derived, not typed.
- `recency_window` — task 72 (AI/4IR) → 2023+ weighted heavily. A 2008-crisis question → 2007-2012 primary + retrospectives; **a recency bonus there is actively wrong.** Sol's "recency-bonus for ≥2023" is a hardcode.
- `language`, `entity_types`.

Out-of-scope evidence never enters the citable corpus. Tier is a WEIGHT inside scope, never a FILTER (this is what admitted ResNet, the BMJ PRISMA checklist and a 1974 Cognitive Psychology paper into our corpus).

---

### STAGE 2 — RETRIEVAL: aimed by the rubric, not by a keyword (operator FIX 1, FIX 2)
- **Recency comes from date-filtered search, not forward traversal.** The operator's Crossref `filter=type:journal-article,from-pub-date:2024-01-01` measurement retires Sol's #1 risk (which he priced at 35% of sinking the plan). Forward traversal (OpenCitations COCI, which Fable verified live at 1,334 citing DOIs for one anchor) becomes a **bonus lane, not a dependency**. Sol's blocking "Gate 0" is deleted.
- **Anchors are DISCOVERED, not named.** Delete Sol's 15 hand-named papers and Fable's 5. Run a broad first search, keep the on-topic results, rank by citation count, take the top-N as citation-graph seeds. This finds Autor-Levy-Murnane for task 72, KarMMa/CARTITUDE for myeloma, and Mian-Sufi for 2008 — **with the same code.** Citation-graph expansion stays, because keyword search provably cannot find ALM 2003 (its title contains neither "AI" nor "labor market") — but the *seeds* are derived.
- **Query generation is DEFICIT-DRIVEN.** Round 1: queries per unfilled facet. **Round N: queries generated from the judge's own written complaint** (Stage 5). That is the loop no plan closes — Sol's agentic sweep aims at a matrix *he guessed*; ours aims at what the grader *said was missing*.
- **SELECT is an LLM relevance judge against `scope.topic_statement`.** The operator is right that SELECT, not retrieval, is the broken stage: more search with a broken SELECT just yields *recent* junk.
- **STOP when the matrix is full**, not at a paper count. Delete Sol's "110-160 DOIs" and Fable's "120-150 named / ≥60 groundable."

---

### STAGE 3 — EVIDENCE: Fable's extractive card, kept almost intact
Fable's rule is the right one and I keep it verbatim: **the LLM may SELECT character offsets, never COMPOSE spans.** Every card field that will license a claim (`finding`, `N`, `design`, `mechanism`) is a verbatim quote with a char offset into fetched text, or NULL. Missing texture is *narrated* as missing, never filled — a required output field with an absent input is the canonical invention condition.

Three fixes, all confirmed in our code tonight:
1. `scripts/cellcog_composer.py:167` copies `'mechanisms': f.get('mechanisms') or []` **straight from LLM output with no span check**, while `claim`/`span` beside it ARE checked. A hallucinated mechanism string becomes a global citation license — two-hop laundering. **Every mechanism carries its own verbatim span or is dropped.**
2. Sol's lying full-text detector: `journal_corpus_fetch.py` accepted a 548-word Oxford landing page ("CONTACT Name Email…") as FULLTEXT. Guard: ≥1500 words AND title-in-body AND no landing-page chrome.
3. Keep Sol's three honest tiers (QUOTABLE / ABSTRACT-CITABLE / NAMED), all three counts always published. An abstract may not license a mechanism.

**Carried forward from Fable, and it is the best constructive idea in either plan: the CONTRADICTION INDEX.** Pairs of cards whose extractive findings diverge on the same question, tagged with their dimension diffs (`level`, `era`, `population`, `method`). This is the raw material for the adjudication move, and it is completely domain-free (see why_general).

---

### STAGE 4 — COMPOSITION: the criteria ARE the outline
The compiled rubric is assigned to **carriers** by one LLM call: each criterion is owned by a SECTION, a per-section DEVICE, or a GLOBAL property. The outline is the section set. **No engineer writes an outline.**

**The move nobody has made: the section writer's prompt contains the rubric criteria that section owns — VERBATIM, with their `explanation` text and weights.** We write to the grader's own words. This is not gaming: the criteria are a faithful decomposition of the *user's* question, generated from it.

That single change fixes Sol's biggest content error by construction. Sol books 11.5% of the score on a **4IR SPINE** — "compare AI to mechanization/electrification/computerization in every H2." His weight arithmetic is right (0.29×0.10 + 0.32×0.15 + 0.25×0.15 = 0.1145), but Fable's decode proves the content is wrong: **cellcog never once compares AI to the previous three revolutions; the system that builds that comparison table is WhaleCloud, at 0.5396.** The criteria say "Grounding in", "Insightful *Integration* of AI's Role *within*" — not "compare to." cellcog wins those criteria by *critiquing and subordinating* the frame with cited sources. Pass the criterion text verbatim and the composer gets this right without anyone theorising about it.

**The device library (all from Fable's cellcog decode — general rhetorical moves of scholarship, not domain constants):**
- **A dedicated named-syntheses section** whose declared purpose is claims no single source makes, each epistemically graded. This is cellcog's insight engine and is the most likely single explanation for 0.5578 vs the 0.54 pack.
- **ADJUDICATION**: [cited finding A] + [cited finding B] + [uncited, hedged resolution that NAMES the resolving dimension]. Fires directly off the contradiction index.
- **MONOGRAPH WIRING**: internal cross-references (cellcog: 32; bodhi: 0). Cheap, and nobody else does it.
- **ORGANIZING PUZZLE**: the opening stakes a concrete evidentiary tension; the conclusion re-opens and resolves it. Nobody else on the board does this.
- **GAPS NARRATED AS FINDINGS** — uncuttable by any faithfulness gate, reads as mastery.
- **COMPLIANCE PERFORMED AT POINT OF USE** — the instruction is turned into displayed scholarly judgement wherever it binds.
- **EPIGRAMMATIC PARAGRAPH CLOSES** — evidence → evidence → punchline.

**Length is DERIVED** (facet count × depth), not the hardcoded 13,500-16,500 that Sol demands. bodhi *wins task 72 at 4,361 body words.* Length is a hypothesis, and Experiment 0 tests it before we spend a month on it.

**Citation form:** author + journal name **in prose** (they survive `clean_prompt.py`; [n] markers and the bibliography do not — confirmed, the cleaner strips marks and reference lists and keeps everything else). But Sol's contorted Format D ("Writing in the QJE in 2003, Autor…") is dropped in favour of cellcog's measured workhorse — `Author(s) (YYYY), in the *Journal*, find that…` (73 instances). **Fable's own document contradicts itself on whether narrative parenthetical years survive the cleaner — so we resolve it with one cleaner run, not an argument.** Bibliography stays for provenance.

---

### STAGE 5 — THE INSTRUMENTED JUDGE (`scripts/race_local.py`) — THE CORE OF THE SYSTEM
Fork `deepresearch_bench_race.py`. Change one thing that matters: **stop deleting `llm_output_json`.**

Persist, for every criterion c, every judge sample: `{criterion, analysis, article_1_score, article_2_score, w_dim, w_crit}`.

That yields, per compose, at k=5:
- **`T_c`** — our score, 0-10, per criterion, with an SE.
- **`R_c`** — the reference's score. Gives the calibration *and* the analytic ceiling 10/(10+R_total).
- **`analysis`** — the judge, in prose, saying exactly why. ("Article 1 covers manufacturing and healthcare but omits agriculture and the public sector; Article 2 covers eight.")

**THE DEFICIT MAP: rank criteria by `priority(c) = W_c × (10 − T_c)`** — weight times headroom. That is the exact first-order gradient of the objective, and it is the entire planning signal. It tells retrieval what to fetch, the composer what to write, and the wheel what to fix — with no human guessing.

**This is the operator's NO PROXIES rule finally satisfied.** We have never had the mechanism-level signal; we had a scalar with 25 hidden components and we guessed which one moved. That is the whole history of `fb228dd`, `225b323`, `b3acb72`.

---

### STAGE 6 — THE WHEEL, AND THE ONE EXPERIMENT THAT COMES FIRST

**EXPERIMENT 0 — ORACLE CALIBRATION. Before any retrieval or composer work. ~20 judge calls.**
Score four artifacts we ALREADY HAVE — our rank10 report (0.4382), bodhi's task-72 (0.5441), **cellcog's task-72 artifact**, and the reference — with the instrumented judge, k=5. In one afternoon this delivers:
1. **cellcog's actual task-72 score.** Fable calls its absence "the biggest units error" in the whole project; Sol builds his entire release gate around not knowing it. **It is one run away.** Both plans propose to build for weeks and then discover this.
2. **R_total → the analytic ceiling.** If R = 7.5 the ceiling is 0.571 and cellcog would already sit at ~97% of the maximum — which would mean Sol's 0.5670 gate is *nearly unreachable* and the honest plan is different. We need to know this before we design to it, not after.
3. **The exact 25-row diff between us and #1.** Nobody has ever seen it. It converts "improve insight" into "criterion I4, Emergent Themes, w=0.20: we score 4.1, cellcog scores 8.6."
4. **The visibility-to-value conversion rate** — Fable's named load-bearing unknown — *measured*, not assumed. Does cellcog's epistemic-tag machinery actually show up as points on the insight criteria? Does bodhi's 4,361 words beat our 8,055 on comprehensiveness? If yes, **length is not the lever** and Sol's word target is wrong.
5. **The per-criterion noise SD**, which tells us whether the deficit map is trustworthy — the one assumption my design rests on, measured on day one.

**THE WHEEL:** deficit map → fix the top-priority criterion → compose → score k=5 instrumented → deficit map re-ranks → repeat.
**KEEP A LEVER iff its TARGETED criterion's T_c rises beyond its k=5 standard error.** Not the overall score — which, as shown above, mathematically cannot resolve a single lever.

**RELEASE GATE: beat cellcog's MEASURED task-72 score (from Experiment 0, k=5, same judge, same reference) by ≥ +0.0094, and report our position as a fraction of the measured ceiling 10/(10+R_total).** This is not bar-lowering — it is the same target both plans want, with the guess replaced by a measurement. Sol's 0.5670 and Fable's 0.5672 are estimates stacked on a regression with a residual SD (0.0094) as large as the effect they are trying to resolve.

---

### CRITICAL-PATH BUG FIXES (all three confirmed by me tonight, all still live)
1. `scripts/synthesis_contract.py`'s `validate()` is **imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and called nowhere.** The only thing between LLM prose and the page is `_clean()`, a regex strip — which the composer then *prints* as "sentences DROPPED by the contract." The safety contract is not on the critical path. **CI assertion: a poisoned card must produce zero poisoned sentences on the page.**
2. `cellcog_composer.py:167` — the unchecked `mechanisms` field (above).
3. The hard-coded abstract at `cellcog_composer.py:400-414` asserts "draws exclusively on peer-reviewed… journal articles identified through citation-graph expansion" **regardless of what the corpus is.** That is a fabricated compliance claim aimed at the instruction-following grader. It dies. The abstract is derived from admitted cards; the Scope section may state only what a corpus audit proves at compose time.
input.why_general:
Nothing in the design names a domain. Here is the walkthrough, and I will name the hardcodes honestly at the end rather than pretend there are none.

## QUERY A — "CAR-T vs bispecific antibodies in relapsed myeloma"

- **DERIVED — the rubric.** RACE's generator, handed only this prompt, produces criteria of the shape it produces for the real medical tasks in the benchmark (I checked tasks 75/76/78: their criteria are literally "Coverage of Clinical Evidence for Intervention Efficacy," "Response to Query on Warning Signs by Disease Stage"). Expect: efficacy (ORR/CR/mPFS/OS/MRD), safety (CRS/ICANS/infection), patient selection and line of therapy, access/manufacturing/vein-to-vein time, evidence quality given **no head-to-head RCT exists**, sequencing after BCMA exposure.
- **DERIVED — the weights.** Across the benchmark these range insight 0.11–0.42, IF 0.13–0.35, readability 0.10–0.25. A comparative treatment-decision question will pull insight up. The system reallocates effort automatically because it *reads* the weights.
- **DERIVED — venue class.** The prompt contains no "journal articles only" instruction, so no hard journal gate; the scope deriver returns a clinical evidence hierarchy (pivotal trials, guidelines, registries). **Sol's blanket journal-only rule, hardcoded from task 72's prompt text, would be wrong here.**
- **DERIVED — recency.** Both modalities are new → 2021+ (KarMMa-3, CARTITUDE-4, MajesTEC-1 era).
- **DERIVED — anchors.** Broad search → on-topic filter → rank by citations → the pivotal trials surface as seeds. Nobody typed "CARTITUDE."
- **DERIVED — the matrix.** Facets per criterion: {ORR, CR, mPFS, OS, MRD} × {CAR-T, bispecific}. Cells fill from cards.
- **THE ADJUDICATION MOVE FIRES, UNCHANGED.** The contradiction index finds CARTITUDE-4's PFS diverging from real-world registry outcomes. The resolving dimension is `population` (trial-eligible vs frail/real-world) — **the same card field, the same code path** as cellcog's firm-vs-worker resolution. The synthesis: *"If [trial PFS, cited] and [registry PFS, cited], then manufacturing lead time may itself select for fitter patients, confounding cross-modality comparison"* — hedged, tagged, carrying two premise_ids, containing no number or entity absent from those premises.
- **THE GAP CLAIM FIRES, MACHINE-VERIFIED.** *"Among the 84 articles retrieved for this review, none reports a randomized head-to-head comparison."* True by construction — it is a query over our own card index, not a claim about the world.
- **4IR does not appear** — not because we suppressed it, but because it was never in the code. It was a criterion in task 72's generated rubric and simply is not generated for this prompt.

## QUERY B — "What caused the 2008 financial crisis"

- **DERIVED — the rubric.** Proximate causes (subprime origination, MBS/CDO, ratings agencies, leverage, shadow banking/repo), structural causes (global imbalances, monetary policy, deregulation), competing schools of explanation, counterfactuals, contested attribution.
- **DERIVED — the weights.** A causal-attribution question with almost no constraints: insight goes high (the benchmark's range tops out at 0.41 on exactly this kind of task — task 3 is 0.41), instruction-following goes **low** (0.17-ish; there is almost nothing to comply with). **The system stops spending on compliance carriers because the grader stopped paying for them.** No plan that hardcodes 0.32/0.29/0.25/0.14 can do this.
- **DERIVED — venue class: UNRESTRICTED.** The FCIC Report, BIS/IMF working papers, Gorton, Bernanke, Rajan are all admissible. **This is the case that proves the point: Sol's hardcoded journal-only gate would delete the single most important primary source in the field (the FCIC Report) because it read a constraint off *task 72's prompt* and baked it into the system.**
- **DERIVED — recency: INVERTED.** Primary sources 2007-2012, plus retrospectives. **Sol's "recency-bonus for ≥2023" would systematically down-weight the entire primary literature.** Recency is a *scope parameter read from the question*, not a constant.
- **DERIVED — anchors.** Search → on-topic → most-cited → Mian & Sufi, Gorton, Bernanke-Gertler, Reinhart-Rogoff, the FCIC report. Nobody typed them.
- **THE ADJUDICATION MOVE FIRES ON A REAL, FAMOUS CONTRADICTION.** Mian-Sufi (credit expansion to marginal/subprime borrowers drove it) vs Adelino-Schoar-Severino (it was middle-class speculation, not subprime). The contradiction index surfaces the pair; the resolving dimension is `level` — **ZIP-code aggregates vs individual-loan microdata.** That is *literally the same resolving dimension* (unit of observation) as cellcog's China firm-vs-worker adjudication, reached by the same code, in a field 30 years and one discipline away. I regard this as the strongest available proof that the mechanism is general rather than a labour-economics trick: **the epistemic move — "the parallel is not a contradiction but a demonstration; the choice of unit of observation is not an innocent methodological decision" — is domain-free, and it is exactly what earns INSIGHT.**

## WHAT IS ACTUALLY HARDCODED — stated honestly
1. **The four RACE dimensions and the 0-10 scale.** These are the benchmark's, not ours. In a non-benchmark deployment the rubric compiler still runs; only the reference must be self-generated (see below).
2. **The four sentence classes (REPORT / COMPARE / ANALYSIS / GAP).** These are epistemological categories, not domain categories. They are the same for myeloma and for 2008.
3. **The card's dimension SLOTS (`level`, `horizon`, `method`, `population`, `era`).** The slots are generic study-descriptors; the VALUES are derived. For 2008, `level` = {loan, ZIP, bank, nation} and `era` = {pre-crisis, crisis, post-crisis}. For myeloma, `level` = {patient, arm, trial, registry}. The slot names are the only thing an engineer wrote, and they are the standard descriptors of any empirical study in any field.
4. **The device library** (adjudication, gap-narration, epigrammatic close, monograph wiring, organizing puzzle, compliance-at-point-of-use). These are rhetorical moves of *scholarship*, not of AI-and-labour. Whether each device is *used* is decided by carrier assignment against the derived criteria.

## THE ONE PLACE GENERALITY IS AT RISK, AND THE FIX
The loop needs a **reference to diff against**. On DRB the true reference ships with the benchmark, so using it makes our local oracle *faithful*. On a novel question there is none — so the system **generates its own baseline** with one fast single-pass report and diffs against that. The mechanism is identical either way: *diff against a baseline, read the per-criterion deficit, spend effort where the weighted headroom is largest.* To keep this honest and prevent the loop degenerating into task-72 overfitting, I impose a **hard code boundary: the composer never reads `reference.jsonl`.** It consumes only the judge's criterion-level scores and analysis text. That boundary is enforceable, testable, and is the line between "reproducing the metric" and "writing to one specific opponent."

**The executable proof, and I want it run before we commit:** run the rubric compiler over all 100 benchmark prompts, measure semantic recall against the real `criteria.jsonl`, and run the full pipeline end-to-end on 3 diverse tasks (the repo already has query_task75/76/78/90 staged). A system that only answers task 72 is a demo; this one is checkable on 100.
input.faithfulness_position:
## THE LINE: no sentence may put words in a source's mouth. That is the moat, and it is NOT "every sentence must be span-grounded."

We have been enforcing the wrong rule, and it is costing us the heaviest-weighted dimension (insight, 0.32 here, and a mean of 0.352 across all 100 tasks — **insight is the heaviest dimension on the benchmark, on average**). `entailment_judge.py:588` deletes any sentence introducing a fact, entity, or mechanism not present in *the span*. That deletes every cross-source inference **by construction**. Meanwhile the #1 system carries no citation on 71% of its sentences and its own insight section opens by declaring it will make claims "that no single paper in the peer-reviewed literature articulates."

## WHAT WE KEEP — the fabrication floor, non-negotiable

1. **No invented number, study, entity, or attribution. Ever.** Every number traces to a verbatim extractive span with a character offset. Fable's rule stands exactly: **the LLM may SELECT offsets, never COMPOSE spans.** An LLM-written field may never serve as the ground truth for the number gate.
2. **The attribution binding gate.** Every author surname and journal name in prose must string-match a card the sentence cites. An off-by-one binding is a false claim about a named real person — it never ships.
3. **The mechanism pool is the union of the SENTENCE'S OWN cited cards** — not the corpus. Fable is right and this beat the operator's first formulation: a corpus-wide pool over 140 cards degenerates `synthesis_contract.py:197-205` into "does the sentence contain a word the field uses," and **flips our own ATTACK #1 from REJECT to PASS.** bodhi never needed the widening — its bridge sentence already cites the mechanism's paper. ATTACK #1 stays REJECTED as a CI canary on every commit.
4. **Every mechanism carries its own verbatim span.** Closes the two-hop laundering hole at `cellcog_composer.py:167`.
5. **`validate()` goes ON the critical path.** Today it is imported at `cellcog_composer.py:49` and called nowhere; the only filter is a regex the composer *advertises* as "the contract." The repo has shipped this bug twice (`225b323`, `b3acb72`). Third time it gets a CI test: a poisoned card must produce zero poisoned sentences on the page.
6. **Gap claims are corpus-scoped.** "Within the 137 articles retrieved for this review, none measures X" — **machine-verifiable against our own card index**, true by construction, identical scoring payoff. cellcog's unscoped "No peer-reviewed study has yet measured X" is a false universal over 10⁴ papers asserted from a convenience sample. We get the points without the lie. A regex gate rejects the unscoped form.

## WHAT WE DROP — the rules that ban thinking, not fabrication

1. **The union-entailment gate** (every assertion literally present in the union of cited spans). It kills bodhi's winning sentence, whose two halves are each fully grounded.
2. **`entailment_judge.py:588`'s NEUTRAL clause as a deletion router.** NEUTRAL becomes rewrite-and-re-judge (max 2), then class-routing, then delete.
3. **The requirement that every sentence carry a citation.** This is the big one. It is the difference between a report that catalogues and one that thinks.

## WHAT MAKES THE RELAXATION SAFE — the four classes, and one crisp test

Every sentence declares its class in a ledger (not inferred, not guessed):
- **REPORT** — entailed by an extractive span of a cited card.
- **COMPARE** — a relation between ≥2 cited findings, each component entailed by its own card.
- **ANALYSIS** — **the reviewer's own reasoning. MAY assert what no source states.** Licensed only by: (a) `premise_ids` of ≥2 admitted cards; (b) **no number and no proper noun that is not in a premise card** — so the no-new-number and no-new-entity gates STILL RUN; (c) hedged; (d) epistemically tagged.
- **GAP** — corpus-scoped, auto-verified against the card index.

**Fable's sharpest single insight, and I am adopting it as the load-bearing safety property: HEDGE + TAG ALONE IS COSTUME. The premises are the license.** An "analytical synthesis" with no premise_ids is a hallucination wearing a lab coat, and routing NEUTRAL verdicts into a premise-free class-4 is exactly how you publish one.

**THE ONE TEST THAT DEFINES THE LINE, and I want it in CI:**
> **Strip every ANALYSIS sentence from the report. Every remaining assertion must be span-traceable. And no ANALYSIS sentence may contain a number or a proper noun absent from its own premises.**

Deterministic, checkable, and it permits *everything* cellcog does while forbidding *everything* fraud does. "We propose, as analytical synthesis, that informality functions as the Global South's institutional analogue to coordinated bargaining" passes — it invents no number, names no study that did not say what we say it said. "The undetectable aggregate effect is *driven by* the reinstatement effect [citing a 2019 robot-theory paper]" fails — it puts a causal claim about a 2022 generative-AI null into a source's mouth.

## WHAT PROTECTS A PRODUCT PEOPLE TRUST vs WHAT MERELY COSTS SCORE

**Protects trust:** the number gate, the entity gate, the attribution binding gate, the mechanism-span gate, corpus-scoped gaps, and the ledger. A reader who checks any citation finds the source says what we said it says. **A 0.60 obtained by fabricating is a 0.00 — and it is worse than a 0.00, because it is a product that lies to a researcher who trusts it.** Any single fabricated number, entity, or attribution found in the line-by-line READ burns the draft regardless of score. That stays.

**Merely costs score:** the demand that the reviewer never reason. That is not a safety property — it is a *capability* deficit dressed as one. It has never prevented a single fabrication (our fabrication holes are at `:167` and `:49`, in code, not in prose policy). It has only guaranteed that we catalogue while cellcog thinks, on the dimension that carries the most weight on this benchmark. **Marked reasoning is the most honest thing a review can do; unmarked reasoning is the fraud. We have been banning the marked kind and shipping the unmarked kind through an unwired gate.**

input.expected_gain:
**I am deliberately NOT giving you a point estimate for the final score, and that is the most important thing in this section.**

Both plans do give one (Sol: 0.563 / 0.571 best-of-3; Fable: 0.572). Both are built by assigning per-dimension deltas by judgement and summing them. The repo's own commit log (`fb228dd`, `225b323`, `b3acb72`, `e87f52e`) is a graveyard of exactly that arithmetic. And I can now show it is arithmetically unsafe: **both gates require T/R = 1.31, and no system has ever exceeded 1.27 on any single dimension of this task.** A plan whose central number sits above the observed frontier, resting on a regression estimate with a residual SD equal to the effect being measured, is not a forecast — it is a hope with decimal places.

## WHAT I WILL COMMIT TO — measurable, and measured within one day

**Experiment 0 (~20 judge calls, one afternoon) delivers, with certainty:**
1. **cellcog's actual task-72 score** — the number both plans build their gates around and neither can see. We have the artifact, the reference, and the judge. It is a run, not a regression.
2. **R_total → the analytic ceiling, 10/(10+R_total).** If R = 7.0 the ceiling is 0.588 and there is real air above cellcog. If R = 7.5 the ceiling is 0.571 and cellcog already sits at ~97% of the maximum — **in which case Sol's 0.5670 gate is very nearly unreachable and the honest plan is a different plan.** We are currently designing without knowing which world we are in.
3. **The exact 25-row per-criterion diff between us, bodhi, and cellcog.** This converts "improve insight (+0.13)" into "criterion I4 (Emergent Themes, W=0.064): we score 4.1, cellcog scores 8.6, headroom 5.9 — this one criterion is worth ~+0.017 alone."
4. **The per-criterion noise SD** — which validates or destroys the deficit map, i.e. the one assumption my whole design rests on, on day one.
5. **Fable's load-bearing unknown, VISIBILITY-to-VALUE, measured rather than assumed.**

## THE GAIN I ACTUALLY CLAIM

Not a score. A **rate**. The deficit map makes each wheel turn ~10x more statistically informative at identical judge cost, because the lever is measured where it acts (its own criterion, on a 0-10 scale, moving 1-3 points at 3-8 sigma) instead of where it is invisible (a 0-1 aggregate, moving ~0.005 against a 0.0094 detection floor at 0.6 sigma).

**That is the whole difference between a project that converges and a project that has burned four nights on levers it could not see.** Every "the mechanism never operated" post-mortem in this repo is the signature of a team optimising a scalar with 25 hidden components. We do not need a better guess about which component to move. We need to stop deleting the vector.

**Honest framing of the headroom:** getting from 0.4382 to ~0.5578 requires our weighted mean criterion score to rise from roughly the "4-6: average, basically meets requirements" band into the "8-10: excellent, fully meets or exceeds" band on nearly every one of 25 criteria, against a competent 9,029-word reference. That is a large, real lift. It is achievable — five systems have done it — but nobody should be told it is comfortable, and the ratio-units framing in both plans quietly conceals how large it is.
honest_risks: THE CEILING MAY BE NEARLY CLOSED. Max achievable = 10/(10+R_total). If the reference scores ~7.5/10, the ceiling is 0.571 and cellcog's ~0.556 already sits at ~97% of the theoretical maximum — leaving almost no air, and making BOTH Sol's 0.5670 and Fable's 0.5672 gates effectively unreachable regardless of how good the report is. This is the single most sobering possibility in the project, no one has considered it, and it is ONE RUN from being known. If it is true, the honest response is to change the plan, not the narrative.

THE PER-CRITERION SIGNAL MAY BE TOO NOISY TO STEER BY. The judge scores all ~25 criteria in ONE call, so the criteria are correlated by a single halo — a good draft lifts everything, a bad one sinks everything. If per-criterion SD is large relative to the deficits, the deficit map is a noise map and the central claim of my design (10x statistical power) collapses toward the aggregate. Mitigation: the paired design cancels much of the halo, and k=5 gives per-criterion SE directly. But I am ASSUMING this, not asserting it — Experiment 0 measures it on day one, and if it fails I need a different sensor.

THE RUBRIC COMPILER MAY NOT REPRODUCE THE REAL CRITERIA. The keystone is that we can regenerate the grader's rubric from the prompt. If semantic recall against the real `criteria.jsonl` is poor, the derived matrix is aimed at the wrong targets and the whole edifice is built on sand. Measurable on all 100 tasks before a single line of the composer is written; fallback is the static rubric in `score_prompt_en.py:104-194` as a floor. But note the failure mode is silent and seductive: a plausible-looking wrong rubric would send retrieval, composition and the wheel confidently in the wrong direction.

OPTIMISING TO THE GRADER'S OWN CRITERION TEXT MAY PRODUCE A RUBRIC-SHAPED DOCUMENT WITH NO SOUL. The faithfulness gates prevent fraud; they do not prevent blandness. cellcog likely wins partly on things no criterion names — a thesis, an organizing puzzle, epigrammatic closes, the sense of one argued monograph. A checklist that satisfies 25 criteria and argues nothing could plausibly score WORSE than a document with a real thesis, because the judge writes its comparative analysis BEFORE it assigns numbers. Mitigation: THESIS is a global carrier and the READ step is mandatory — but I cannot prove this risk away, and it is the one my architecture is most structurally exposed to.

READING THE JUDGE'S OUTPUT IS ONE STEP FROM WRITING TO ONE SPECIFIC OPPONENT. The loop consumes criterion-level analysis; the temptation to let the composer see `reference.jsonl` and target its specific gaps is exactly how a general system decays into a task-72 machine. I impose a hard code boundary (the composer never reads the reference) — but it is a discipline, not a physical law, and it will be under pressure every time the score stalls.

LENGTH MAY BE A TRAP IN EITHER DIRECTION. cellcog is 13,580 words and #1 corpus-wide; bodhi is 4,361 and #1 on task 72. Sol bets everything on the cellcog reading. I bet on neither and let Experiment 0 arbitrate — but if the answer is task-dependent (long where the reference is long, short where it is short), then a single derived length rule is wrong and I will need a per-task length policy I have not designed.

REFERENCE LENGTH VARIES 494 TO 12,756 WORDS ACROSS TASKS, so the achievable ceiling is wildly task-dependent and corpus-wide rank is won disproportionately on tasks where the reference is weak. My design optimises a single task well; it does not yet contain a BUDGET POLICY for allocating effort across 100 tasks of very different winnability. That is a real gap between 'beats SOTA on task 72' and 'beats SOTA corpus-wide', and I have not closed it.

THE FABRICATION FLOOR HAS NEVER ACTUALLY BEEN ENFORCED IN THIS REPO. `validate()` has been imported-and-never-called through at least two shipped 'fixes' (commits 225b323, b3acb72). I am proposing to RELAX the faithfulness rules while relying on gates that have a documented history of looking armed and never firing. If the CI poisoned-card test is not the very first thing built and kept green, the relaxation is not scholarship — it is just an unlocked door.

27 OF THE 100 TASKS ARE IN CHINESE (I verified: criteria are generated in Chinese via `criteria_prompt_zh.py`, and reference articles for those tasks run as short as 494 words). Nothing in my design breaks on this — language is derived — but nothing in it has been TESTED on this either, and no plan in this project has ever mentioned it.
